data.title: Strategy Stock Hits 52-Week Low Amid Bitcoin Price Decline
data.description: Strategy stock (MSTR) has reached its lowest point in 52 weeks following a significant drop of over 6% in Bitcoin's price, with the cryptocurrency now trading around $84,300. According to NS3.AI, despite this downturn, Strategy has announced a substantial purchase of 2,932 BTC for $264 million. This acquisition increases the company's total Bitcoin holdings to over 712,000 BTC. Strategy continues to utilize at-the-market offerings to finance its Bitcoin purchases, currently holding approximately 3.4% of the total Bitcoin supply.

data.title: U.S. SEC and CFTC to Sign Agreement on Cryptocurrency Regulation
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) are set to sign a Memorandum of Understanding to enhance coordination in regulating the cryptocurrency industry. According to NS3.AI, the two agencies are working together to define their supervisory roles and jurisdiction over crypto assets. Detailed plans regarding this collaboration will be unveiled at a joint event scheduled for January 30th.

data.title: APAC Leads Institutional Digital Asset Adoption with Clear Regulations
data.description: The Asia-Pacific (APAC) region is at the forefront of institutional digital asset adoption, driven by clear and compliance-focused regulatory frameworks. According to NS3.AI, countries like Hong Kong, Singapore, and Japan are facilitating significant institutional capital flows by implementing secure, phased licensing and sandbox environments. These measures aim to balance innovation with regulatory oversight. This structured, principles-based approach sets APAC apart from other regions facing regulatory uncertainty, establishing it as a global leader in integrating digital assets into traditional finance.

data.title: Bitcoin Upside Hinges On Liquidity Rising Above This Level, Glassnode Says
data.description:
A report from on-chain analytics firm Glassnode has highlighted how transitions into strong upside phases have historically required liquidity to hold above a key threshold. Bitcoin Rally Could Require Realized Profit/Loss Ratio To Rise Above 5 In its latest weekly report, Glassnode has talked about liquidity conditions present on the Bitcoin network as the asset’s price has gone through a drawdown following its failed recovery attempt earlier in the month. Related Reading: Bitcoin Death Cross That Last Preceded A 66% Drop Is Back “Any meaningful transition back toward a sustained rally should objectively be reflected in liquidity-sensitive indicators such as the Realized Profit/Loss Ratio (90D-SMA),” explained the analytics firm. The Realized Profit/Loss Ratio refers to an indicator that, as its name suggests, compares the realized profit and loss that BTC investors realize from their transactions
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/bitcoin-news/bitcoin-liquidity-rising/

data.title: Ethereum Introduces ERC-8004 Standard for AI Agent Identity and Reputation
data.description: Ethereum has unveiled the ERC-8004 standard, which aims to establish a neutral and composable infrastructure for AI agent identity, reputation, and validation on-chain. According to NS3.AI, this new standard seeks to tackle trust issues as AI agents move into production deployments involving real transactions, facilitating interoperation without the need for centralized gatekeepers. Potential adoption scenarios vary, ranging from cautious implementations to extensive registration of millions of agents, alongside the emergence of validation and reputation middleware on Ethereum and Layer 2 chains.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 84,000 USDT with a 6.55% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jan 29, 2026, 18:26 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 84,000 USDT and is now trading at 83,937.109375 USDT, with a narrowed 6.55% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: CFTC Chairman Advocates for Market Structure Bill to Boost U.S. Crypto Industry
data.description: Mike Selig, chairman of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), emphasized the importance of passing a market structure bill to support the growth of the cryptocurrency industry. According to NS3.AI, Selig believes that establishing clear national regulatory standards will encourage blockchain companies to return to the United States, positioning the country as a leading hub for cryptocurrency activities. He acknowledged the significant impact blockchain technology has had on market development within the agency's jurisdiction over the last 15 years.

data.title: U.S. Senate Rejects House Spending Bill, Heightening Shutdown Concerns
data.description: The U.S. Senate has turned down a spending bill approved by the House, intensifying concerns over a potential partial government shutdown. According to NS3.AI, this procedural vote has heightened uncertainty regarding the continuity of government functions. Market participants are now closely monitoring the situation as the likelihood of a shutdown grows.

data.title: 43% of Wall Street experts see prediction markets adding value, yet caution on trading depth
data.description:

The work was produced by Crisil Coalition Greenwich and focuses on how these markets may fit into professional trading over […]
